source_filing: Walmart Inc. Form 10-K (fiscal year ended January 31, 2026)
original_text: The increases were primarily due to strong positive comparable sales across our U.S. segments and international markets. In fiscal 2026, growth was primarily driven by increases in average ticket and transactions,
modified_text: Across all of Walmart's reportable segments worldwide, net sales growth in fiscal 2026 was primarily driven by increases in average ticket and transactions.
claim: All of Walmart's reportable segments experienced net sales growth driven by increases in average ticket and transactions in fiscal 2026.
citation_as_written: Walmart Inc. Form 10-K (fiscal year ended January 31, 2026), Results of Operations
notes: Source attributes growth to 'strong positive comparable sales across our U.S. segments and international markets' — a description of consolidated drivers, not a claim that every reportable segment individually grew via ticket and transactions. The claim generalizes the ticket/transaction driver to all segments worldwide, expanding the scope beyond what the passage states.
